task_completion.description: Create a draw.io diagram with two pages named 'Level 1 DFD' and 'STRIDE Analysis'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface should show two page tabs at the bottom, specifically named 'Level 1 DFD' and 'STRIDE Analysis'.

task_completion.description: Include all required components in the Level 1 DFD
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Level 1 DFD' page should contain shapes representing Browser/User, Stripe Payment Gateway, SMTP Email Service, Angular SPA, Express.js API, Authentication Module, SQLite Database, and File System.

task_completion.description: Include trust boundaries and labeled data flows in the Level 1 DFD
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Level 1 DFD' page should contain trust boundaries (e.g., dashed boxes or lines) labeled Internet, Application, and Data, along with arrows connecting the components that have text labels describing the data flow.

task_completion.description: Identify at least 6 potential threats mapped to STRIDE categories
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'STRIDE Analysis' page should contain text, a list, or a table identifying at least 6 distinct threats, each mapped to one of the STRIDE categories (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege).

task_completion.description: Save the diagram as juice_shop_threat_model.drawio on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io window title should show 'juice_shop_threat_model.drawio', or the file should be visible on the Desktop in a file manager.

task_completion.description: Export the Level 1 DFD page as a PNG image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'juice_shop_threat_model.png' should be visible on the Desktop, or an image viewer/export dialog should show the successful export of the DFD page as a PNG.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io (diagrams.net) application interface being used to drag and drop shapes, type text, and create connections, rather than a script generating the XML.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the architecture specification file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening '~/Desktop/juice_shop_architecture.md' in a text editor or terminal to read the system details.

integrity.description: The diagram was created manually and not just pasted as a single image
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface should show individual selectable shapes and connectors, not a single imported image of a DFD.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required components for the DFD are: Browser/User, Stripe Payment Gateway, SMTP Email Service, Angular SPA, Express.js API, Authentication Module, SQLite Database, and File System. The required trust boundaries are: Internet, Application, and Data. The STRIDE categories are: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege. The diagram must have at least 6 shapes, 8 data flow edges, and 2 trust boundaries.
